sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute two reimbursement
agreements and any amendments with Union Pacific Railroad Company (UPRR) to
construct all necessary safety improvement elements at Addison Street and Bancroft
Way railroad crossings subject to reimbursement by the City for actual costs incurred.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
There will be no fiscal impact to the City. The developers of the projects triggering the
safety improvements are responsible for all actual costs and expenses for the UPRR
parties performance of the safety improvement work at the two railroad crossings. There
are separate agreements with the two developers regarding payment of actual costs of
the safety improvements
sections.current_situation:
Berkeley Commons is the owner of 600 Addison, and Steelwave Development is the
owner of 747 Bancroft. Their projects are next to the railroad crossings at Addison
Street and Bancroft Way. Berkeley Commons is constructing the Berkeley Commons
research and development campus, which includes two buildings with a combined gross
floor area of 470,986 square feet and two parking structures providing a total of 943
spaces on an approximately 8.5-acre industrial site. Steelwave is constructing a new
development at 747 Bancroft Way, which consists of a 159,143-square-foot, three-story
building containing 124,539 square feet of research and development space and 34,604
square feet of light manufacturing space, and a surface parking lot containing 76 off-
street parking spaces and five loading spaces.
Both of these developments are subject to certain conditions of approval which requires
them to coordinate with the UPRR, the California Public Utilities commission (CPUC)
and the City to implement safety improvements of all travelers at the railroad crossings
at Addison and Bancroft.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 91
Agreements with Union Pacific Railroad for Reimbursement CONSENT CALENDAR
for Construction of Safety Improvements for Pedestrians, Cyclists January 21, 2025
and Vehicular Traffic at Addison and Bancroft railroad crossings
The mitigation measures for the two projects will result in safety improvements for all
modes of travel across the Addison Street and Bancroft Way railroad crossings.
Executing this agreement will support the City's Strategic Plan Goal of creating a
resilient, safe, connected, and prepared City.
sections.background:
Developers are responsible for the need to implement project-triggered safety
improvements across railroad crossings. UPRR has a strict policy of being in charge of
implementing any work along their operating corridor, in this case those triggered by the
two developments across railroad crossings at Addison Street and Bancroft Way. UPRR
does not enter into agreements with private entities, therefore in order to support the
development projects and implementation of these safety improvements the City will
enter into an agreement with the UPRR on behalf of the developers with the developers
agreeing to pay all the final UPRR costs associated with these improvements. Separate
agreements between the city and the two developers regarding their payment of the
final costs of the safety improvements have already been completed.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
In light of the upcoming research and development employment centers near the
Addison and Bancroft railroad crossings, it is anticipated that implementation of the
safety improvements will encourage non-motorized traffic. This is expected to have a
positive impact on the environment.
sections.rationale:
In order to construct required safety improvements within the UPRR operating corridor,
a construction and maintenance agreement is required by UPRR as well as CPUC.
sections.alternatives:
UPRR requires a construction and maintenance agreement in order to implement safety
improvements on City streets which cross their operating corridor, so no other
alternatives were feasible.
CONTACT PERSON
Wahid Amiri, Deputy Director, Public Works, 510-981-6396

Page 2
Page 3 of 91
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
REIMBURSEMENT AGREEMENTS WITH UNION PACIFIC RAILROAD
WHEREAS, Berkeley Commons is engaged in the development of certain real property
located at 600 Addison Street in City of Berkeley; and
WHEREAS, Steelwave is engaged in the development of certain real property located
at 747 Bancroft Way in City of Berkeley; and
WHEREAS, As part of the improvements for these two projects, safety improvements
need to be constructed at Addison Street/Union Pacific Railroad crossing and at
Bancroft Way/Union Pacific Railroad crossing; and
WHEREAS, Berkeley Commons and Steelwave will be responsible for paying for the
costs of the safety improvements; and
WHEREAS, Union Pacific Railroad (UPRR) will be constructing the safety
improvements at Addison Street and at Bancroft Way; and
WHEREAS, In order for the construction of these required improvements to proceed,
the City must to enter into Construction and Maintenance agreements with UPRR;
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that City
Manager is authorized to execute two reimbursement agreements (Attachments 1 and
2) and any amendments with UPRR to reimburse UPRR actual costs incurred in
connection with traffic safety improvement work at Addison St. and at Bancroft Way
intersections with UPRR crossings.
